title: [CI:DOCS] Cleanup CNI Networks on reboot
body:
CNI sometimes leaves Network information in /var/lib/cni/networks
when the system crashes or containers do not shut down properly.

This PR will cleanup these left over files, so that container engines
will get a clean enviroment when the system reboots.

Related to: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/3759

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This is really not a DOCS only change, but the CI system does not test this functionality.  Machines require a reboot to make sure this works properly.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

We try to remove these files already, no?
 https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/dd954781e6e308a0bbecfaf6699b41426100a58d/libpod/container_internal.go#L604-L613
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/dd954781e6e308a0bbecfaf6699b41426100a58d/libpod/container_internal.go#L631

I don't think existing code is working. I took a quick look through my `/var/lib/cni/networks/podman` yesterday and had a few hundred orphaned IP address reservation files. That's probably uncommonly high because I'm a developer and actively recreating bugs and such, but it still speaks to our deletion efforts on that front being less than successful.
I also had a ton of them.

@Luap99 @mheon @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
Can we get this merged.  I think this is safe whether or not the other code is correct.  Bottom line is these files can be left around if the machine suddenly crashes, and we want them cleaned up.
